Title: Bacterial type III effector–induced plant C8 volatiles elicit antibacterial immunity in heterospecific neighbouring plants via airborne signalling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid-dependent systemic acquired resistance triggered by 1-octen-3-ol
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: defence signalling linked to oxylipin volatile response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Silencing SABP2 in tobacco disrupted the protective effect of 1-octen-3-ol against Pta. 2. This supports that SA-related signalling is required for C8 volatile-induced immunity in receiver plants.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure of tobacco to synthetic 1-octen-3-ol followed by Pta challenge; TRV-VIGS silencing
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediator of SA-dependent defence priming against bacterial pathogen
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene name appears as NbSABP2/SABP2 in Results and Methods; paper also lists NbSABP1 in VIGS methods, but Results interpret SABP2-related SA signalling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRV-VIGS gene silencing; pathogen population assay after 1-octen-3-ol exposure
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to maintain responsiveness to airborne defence volatiles in disease-resistance engineering

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid-dependent systemic acquired resistance triggered by 1-octen-3-ol
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: defence signalling linked to oxylipin volatile response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Silencing NbICS disrupted the protective effect of 1-octen-3-ol on tobacco against Pta. 2. Indicates SA biosynthesis is required for volatile-induced resistance.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to synthetic 1-octen-3-ol; Pta challenge; VIGS silencing
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive requirement for 1-octen-3-ol-induced antibacterial resistance via SA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports SA production for defence priming
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in receiver plant signalling rather than volatile biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRV-VIGS gene silencing; pathogen population assay

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid-dependent systemic acquired resistance triggered by 1-octen-3-ol
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: defence signalling linked to oxylipin volatile response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Silencing NbNPR1 disrupted 1-octen-3-ol-mediated protection against Pta. 2. Demonstrates requirement of canonical SA signalling for airborne volatile-induced SAR.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to synthetic 1-octen-3-ol; Pta challenge; VIGS silencing
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Receiver-side signalling component, not direct volatile biosynthetic gene.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRV-VIGS gene silencing; pathogen population assay

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid-dependent systemic acquired resistance triggered by 1-octen-3-ol
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: defence signalling linked to oxylipin volatile response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Silencing NbSIPK disrupted the protective effect of 1-octen-3-ol on tobacco against Pta. 2. Supports involvement of SA-associated defence signalling in response to the volatile.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to synthetic 1-octen-3-ol; Pta challenge; VIGS silencing
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive requirement for volatile-triggered defence against bacterial infection
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defence signal transduction downstream of volatile perception
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The Methods list NbMEK1 in VIGS candidates, whereas the Results/Figure S3 emphasize NbSIPK; extracted based on Results.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRV-VIGS gene silencing; pathogen population assay

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid-dependent systemic acquired resistance triggered by 1-octen-3-ol
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: defence signalling linked to oxylipin volatile response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Silencing COI1 enhanced the protective effect of 1-octen-3-ol in tobacco. 2. Indicates JA signalling antagonizes the SA-dependent airborne resistance response.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to synthetic 1-octen-3-ol; Pta challenge; VIGS silencing
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of 1-octen-3-ol-induced resistance; its silencing increases protection
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA pathway component antagonizing SA-mediated defence priming
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to establish pathway specificity of the receiver response.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRV-VIGS gene silencing; pathogen population assay

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HopP1 was identified as the strongest effector causing airborne immune activation. 2. HopP1 induced elevated 1-octen-3-ol, 3-octanone and 3-octanol in lima bean. 3. HopP1-triggered volatiles correlated with reduced pathogen growth in tobacco.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Emitter lima bean inoculated with Pseudomonas fluorescens EtHAn expressing HopP1; comparison with Pto wild type, Pf EtHAn empty vector, and T3SS mutants hrcC/hrpL; airborne exposure for 7 days
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes HopP1-dependent emission of C8 volatiles from lima bean that induce systemic acquired resistance in neighbouring tobacco and suppress Pta population
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Airborne defence signals that prime antibacterial immunity/systemic acquired resistance in neighbouring plants
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly associated with activation of SA-responsive defence genes in receiver tobacco, including NbPR1a, NbPR1c, NbHIN1 and NbSAR8.2
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plant cell cytosol after type III translocation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ABR1 (discussed from external study; not validated in this paper)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major effector responsible for inducing the C8 volatile blend linked to airborne immune activation; strongest effector in the screen
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HopP1 is a bacterial type III effector rather than a plant endogenous regulator. The paper proposes unknown plant proteins/enzymes generate the C8 volatiles downstream of HopP1. Structural discussion notes a C-terminal soluble lytic transglycosylase domain.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Effector delivery using Pf EtHAn; pathogen growth assay in receiver tobacco; GC-MS volatile profiling of emitter lima bean
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III secreted effector; harpin-like protein with C-terminal soluble lytic transglycosylase domain (discussed)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Effector-triggered modulation of host volatile emission
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatiles emitted from lima bean leaves into air

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The Pto hrpL mutant failed to induce airborne resistance in neighbouring tobacco. 2. hrpL function was required for induction of SA-dependent defence markers in receiver plants. 3. hrpL mutant-treated lima bean did not show significant increase of the identified C8 volatiles.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison of wild-type Pto versus hrpL mutant inoculation on lima bean; airborne exposure of tobacco
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for T3SS-dependent induction of volatile-mediated immune priming in neighbouring tobacco
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Airborne defence signals that mediate SAR-like antibacterial immunity
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Type III secretion system and downstream effector delivery (functional inference from text)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for effective induction of receiver tobacco defence genes NbPR1a and NbPR1c after volatile exposure
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream regulatory component for effector-dependent volatile induction; loss abolishes phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states hrpL encodes a regulatory component of the T3SS. It is an upstream bacterial virulence regulator, not a plant regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant comparison (Pto wild type vs hrpL mutant); receiver pathogen assay; qRT-PCR of tobacco defence genes; GC-MS volatile profiling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of type III secretion/effector delivery
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatiles emitted from lima bean leaves into air

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The Pto hrcC mutant did not induce significant accumulation of HopP1-associated C8 volatiles in lima bean. 2. This supports a requirement for an intact T3SS in volatile induction.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison with wild-type Pto and other T3SS-defective conditions in lima bean
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for T3SS-dependent induction of C8 volatile emission in lima bean
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports that T3SS structural integrity is essential upstream of volatile induction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results directly test hrcC mutant in volatile induction, though it is not the primary focal effector.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant comparison in GC-MS volatile profiling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Enables effector translocation rather than direct transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatiles emitted from lima bean leaves into air
